messages.content:
I can’t run tasks from here, but I can tell you exactly how to run “load test task 0” on your machine or CI and what I’d need from you to run it myself.

Quick reasoning: I don’t have access to your environment or repository, so I need to know which load-test tool and where the task is defined, plus any required environment variables or target URL.

Direct steps and common commands (pick the one that matches your project):

1) Confirm where the test is defined and which tool:
- Tell me: file path (e.g. tests/task0.js, loadtests/task_0.py), and tool (k6, Locust, Vegeta, ApacheBench, artillery, Gatling, a Make/ npm/Invoke task, or a CI job name).

2) Prepare environment
- Install the tool (example): k6, locust, vegeta, artillery, etc.
- Set required env vars: TARGET_URL, AUTH_TOKEN, or other config used by the script.
- Ensure network access to the target and any test accounts.

3) Example run commands by tool
- k6 (JS):
  - Local: k6 run path/to/task0.js
  - Docker: docker run --rm -v $(pwd)/path/to:/tests -e TARGET_URL=... loadimpact/k6 run /tests/task0.js

- Locust (Python):
  - headless: locust -f path/to/task0.py --headless -u 200 -r 10 -t 5m --host http://TARGET_URL
  - web UI: locust -f path/to/task0.py --host http://TARGET_URL

- Vegeta:
  - echo "GET http://TARGET_URL/endpoint" | vegeta attack -duration=60s -rate=200 | tee results.bin | vegeta report

- ApacheBench (ab):
  - ab -n 10000 -c 100 http://TARGET_URL/endpoint/

- artillery (JS):
  - npx artillery run path/to/task0.yml

- If the test is a project task (Make/npm/invoke)
  - Make: make task0
  - npm: npm run task0
  - invoke: inv task0

4) Monitoring and output
- Capture output to a file (eg. k6 run --out json=results.json ...)
- Monitor system metrics (top, htop, docker stats, Prometheus/Grafana if available)
- Collect server logs and APM traces for failures

5) Example minimal command you can paste in your shell (k6 example):
- export TARGET_URL=https://staging.example.com
- k6 run tests/loadtest_task0.js

What I need from you if you want precise commands or a run:
- Which tool is used and path to the test file (e.g. tests/task0.js)
- Target environment/URL and any necessary env vars or auth (you can give names rather than secret values)
- Whether you want local execution instructions or a CI job (and which CI)

If you want me to change repository files so the task can be run from CI, submit a concrete coding request (or turn off Chat-only mode).

nextActions.label: Continue chat
nextActions.description: Ask a follow-up question in the same thread.
